treatment_information.treatment_response: SOB and chest pain much improved, reports pain on lips from ulcers.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 1000 mg PO every 12 hours for 1 day

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented to PCP with complaints of fatigue and was found to have UTI and anemia.
Patient transferred to OSH after developing severe L sided chest pain.
Underwent pericardiocentesis and placement of pericardial drain.
Patient moved to CCU for post-op monitoring.
Patient reports improvement in SOB and chest pain, with pain on lips from ulcers.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow-up instructions provided, but not specified.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Fluid accumulation likely related to viral infection, but no bacterial or cancer evidence.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient was alert, interactive, and ambulatory at discharge.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient tolerated the procedure well and heart function returned to normal.
